toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# GPU instance user-data bootstrap script
# Installs: Docker Engine, NVIDIA Container Toolkit, CloudWatch Agent, uv, rune
# Runs on first boot via cloud-init on Deep Learning Base GPU AMI (Ubuntu 22.04)
#
# NOTE: This file is processed by OpenTofu templatefile(). All literal dollar-brace
# in bash must be doubled ($$) so templatefile passes them through.
#
# shellcheck disable=SC2154  # Variables are injected by OpenTofu templatefile()
set -euxo pipefail
export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Variables injected by OpenTofu templatefile()
ENVIRONMENT="${environment}"
PROJECT_NAME="${project_name}"
ARTIFACT_BUCKET="${artifact_bucket}"
AWS_REGION="${aws_region}"
WORKSPACE_S3_PREFIX="s3://$ARTIFACT_BUCKET/workspace-snapshots/$ENVIRONMENT"

echo "=== Bootstrapping GPU devbox for $PROJECT_NAME ($ENVIRONMENT) ==="

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Verify NVIDIA drivers (pre-installed in Deep Learning Base GPU AMI)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if command -v nvidia-smi &> /dev/null; then
    echo "NVIDIA driver detected:"
    nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,driver_version,memory.total --format=csv,noheader
else
    echo "WARNING: nvidia-smi not found. GPU drivers may not be installed."
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Docker Engine
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Installing Docker Engine ==="
apt-get update
apt-get install -y jq curl ca-certificates gnupg lsb-release git make

# Install Docker CE if not already present (DLAMI may have containerd pre-installed)
if ! command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
  curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh
fi
systemctl enable docker
systemctl start docker
usermod -aG docker ubuntu || true
usermod -aG docker ssm-user 2>/dev/null || true

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NVIDIA Container Toolkit
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Installing NVIDIA Container Toolkit ==="
distribution=$$(. /etc/os-release; echo "$$ID$$VERSION_ID")
curl -fsSL https://nvidia.github.io/libnvidia-container/gpgkey \
  | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/nvidia-container-toolkit-keyring.gpg
curl -s -L "https://nvidia.github.io/libnvidia-container/$$distribution/libnvidia-container.list" \
  | sed 's#deb https://#deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/nvidia-container-toolkit-keyring.gpg] https://#g' \
  | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/nvidia-container-toolkit.list
apt-get update
apt-get install -y nvidia-container-toolkit
nvidia-ctk runtime configure --runtime=docker --set-as-default
systemctl restart docker

# Verify Docker can see GPU
echo "=== Verifying Docker GPU access ==="
docker run --rm --gpus all nvidia/cuda:12.0.0-base-ubuntu22.04 nvidia-smi || echo "GPU Docker test skipped (no image cached yet)"

# Pre-pull devcontainer base image in background (eliminates ~90s during devpod up)
echo "=== Pre-pulling devcontainer image ==="
docker pull nvidia/cuda:13.0.2-cudnn-devel-ubuntu22.04 &

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CloudWatch Agent
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Installing CloudWatch Agent ==="
mkdir -p /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc

cat > /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json <<'CWCONFIG'
${cloudwatch_agent_config}
CWCONFIG

tmp_deb="/tmp/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb"
curl -fsSL "https://amazoncloudwatch-agent.s3.amazonaws.com/ubuntu/amd64/latest/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb" -o "$$tmp_deb"
dpkg -i "$$tmp_deb"
/opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl \
  -a fetch-config -m ec2 \
  -c file:/opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/etc/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json -s

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSM Agent (usually pre-installed, ensure running)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet amazon-ssm-agent 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Starting SSM agent..."
    snap install amazon-ssm-agent --classic 2>/dev/null || true
    systemctl enable amazon-ssm-agent
    systemctl start amazon-ssm-agent
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SSH Server (for DevPod over SSM tunnel — no inbound SG rules, SSM-only access)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Enabling SSH server ==="
apt-get install -y openssh-server 2>/dev/null || true
systemctl enable ssh
systemctl start ssh

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# uv (Python package manager for rune)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Installing uv ==="
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Make uv available system-wide
cp /root/.local/bin/uv /usr/local/bin/uv 2>/dev/null || true
cp /root/.local/bin/uvx /usr/local/bin/uvx 2>/dev/null || true

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Workspace sync script (save/restore workspace to/from S3)
# Written to /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync with S3 path and region baked in.
# Uses a quoted heredoc so bash doesn't expand variables at write-time;
# placeholders are replaced by sed after writing.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Installing workspace-sync ==="
cat > /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync <<'SYNC_SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

WORKSPACE="/opt/workspace/rune"
S3_PREFIX="__S3_PREFIX__"
SYNC_REGION="__REGION__"
LOGFILE="/var/log/workspace-sync.log"
ACTION="$${1:-save}"

EXCLUDES=(
  --exclude '.git/*'
  --exclude '__pycache__/*'
  --exclude '*.pyc'
  --exclude '.venv/*'
  --exclude 'node_modules/*'
  --exclude '.mypy_cache/*'
  --exclude '.ruff_cache/*'
  --exclude '.pytest_cache/*'
  --exclude '*.egg-info/*'
  --exclude '.cache/*'
)

log() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOGFILE"; }

case "$ACTION" in
  save)
    if [ ! -d "$WORKSPACE" ]; then
      log "No workspace at $WORKSPACE — nothing to save."
      exit 0
    fi
    log "Saving workspace to $S3_PREFIX/latest/"
    aws s3 sync "$WORKSPACE/" "$S3_PREFIX/latest/" \
      "$${EXCLUDES[@]}" --delete --region "$SYNC_REGION" --quiet
    aws s3 cp - "$S3_PREFIX/latest/.sync-metadata" \
      --region "$SYNC_REGION" <<< "synced_at=$(date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')"
    log "Save complete."
    ;;
  restore)
    log "Checking for workspace snapshot at $S3_PREFIX/latest/"
    if aws s3 ls "$S3_PREFIX/latest/.sync-metadata" --region "$SYNC_REGION" &>/dev/null; then
      log "Restoring workspace from S3..."
      mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE"
      aws s3 sync "$S3_PREFIX/latest/" "$WORKSPACE/" \
        --delete --region "$SYNC_REGION" --quiet
      log "Restore complete."
      exit 0
    else
      log "No snapshot found — will do fresh clone."
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  status)
    if aws s3 ls "$S3_PREFIX/latest/.sync-metadata" --region "$SYNC_REGION" &>/dev/null; then
      aws s3 cp "$S3_PREFIX/latest/.sync-metadata" - --region "$SYNC_REGION"
    else
      echo "No workspace snapshot found."
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Usage: workspace-sync [save|restore|status]"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac
SYNC_SCRIPT

# Replace placeholders with actual values (bake S3 path and region into script)
sed -i "s|__S3_PREFIX__|$$WORKSPACE_S3_PREFIX|g" /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync
sed -i "s|__REGION__|$$AWS_REGION|g" /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rune development prerequisites
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Preparing rune development environment ==="

# Create shared workspace directory (accessible by ubuntu and ssm-user)
mkdir -p /opt/workspace
chmod 775 /opt/workspace

# Restore flow: S3 snapshots exclude .git entirely, so we always need a
# valid clone. Restore overlays working-tree files (configs, uncommitted
# changes) on top of a fresh clone.
HAS_SNAPSHOT=false
if /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync restore; then
  HAS_SNAPSHOT=true
  echo "Working tree files restored from S3."
fi

# Always ensure a valid git repo exists
if [ ! -d /opt/workspace/rune/.git ]; then
  if [ "$$HAS_SNAPSHOT" = true ]; then
    # Clone into temp dir, then move .git into restored workspace
    echo "Cloning git repo into restored workspace..."
    git clone --no-checkout https://github.com/ElixirTrials/rune.git /tmp/rune-clone
    mv /tmp/rune-clone/.git /opt/workspace/rune/.git
    rm -rf /tmp/rune-clone
    cd /opt/workspace/rune && git checkout HEAD -- . 2>/dev/null || true
  else
    echo "No S3 snapshot — doing fresh clone..."
    git clone https://github.com/ElixirTrials/rune.git /opt/workspace/rune 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
fi

# Install/update dependencies
if [ -d /opt/workspace/rune ]; then
  cd /opt/workspace/rune
  git pull --ff-only 2>/dev/null || true
  /usr/local/bin/uv sync --all-extras || echo "rune dependency install deferred (run: cd /opt/workspace/rune && uv sync --all-extras)"
fi

# Symlink for both users so ~/workspace/rune works regardless of which user connects
for user_home in /home/ubuntu /home/ssm-user /root; do
  if [ -d "$$user_home" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$$user_home/workspace"
    ln -sfn /opt/workspace/rune "$$user_home/workspace/rune" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
done

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Periodic workspace sync (cron — protects against spot interruptions)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Setting up workspace sync cron ==="
cat > /etc/cron.d/workspace-sync <<'CRON'
*/30 * * * * root /usr/local/bin/workspace-sync save >> /var/log/workspace-sync.log 2>&1
CRON
chmod 644 /etc/cron.d/workspace-sync

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shutdown hook (sync workspace before termination)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "=== Setting up shutdown sync hook ==="
cat > /etc/systemd/system/workspace-sync-shutdown.service <<'SYSTEMD'
[Unit]
Description=Sync workspace to S3 before shutdown
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/true
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/workspace-sync save
RemainAfterExit=yes
TimeoutStopSec=300

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
SYSTEMD
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable workspace-sync-shutdown.service
systemctl start workspace-sync-shutdown.service

content.text:
Here's what's set up:

**Two complementary mechanisms:**

1. **SSM Parameter** (already created): `/elixirtrials/dev/training-data-path`
   - Readable from anywhere (local dev, CI, other services)
   - `aws ssm get-parameter --name /elixirtrials/dev/training-data-path --query Parameter.Value --output text --region eu-west-2`

2. **Devpod env var** (edit in infra repo): `/etc/profile.d/rune-training.sh`
   - Automatically exports `RUNE_TRAINING_DATA` in every shell session on boot
   - Uses the existing `$ARTIFACT_BUCKET` variable (no SSM call needed at runtime)
   - `$$ARTIFACT_BUCKET` → OpenTofu templatefile passes through as `$ARTIFACT_BUCKET` → bash expands to `elixirtrials-949678234935-eu-west-2-artifacts`

On the devpod, usage is just:
```bash
echo $RUNE_TRAINING_DATA
# s3://elixirtrials-949678234935-eu-west-2-artifacts/training-data/github-pairs

aws s3 ls $RUNE_TRAINING_DATA/
aws s3 sync $RUNE_TRAINING_DATA/ ./local-training-data/
```

The infra edit is in `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/infra/providers/aws/files/gpu-userdata.sh`. Want me to commit it, or do you handle infra repo commits separately?

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: GitHub trajectory mining pipeline for hypernetwork training" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **d2l_diff.py** — RTK-style diff compression: filters lockfiles, generated code, binary files, and build artifacts from mined diffs to minimize token overhead in training data
- **normalize_mined_pairs()** — Extracts per-step training pairs from mined PR trajectories (review feedback → code revision cycles), producing `{task_id, activation_text, teacher_text, metadata}` records compatible with the existing distillation pipeline
- **Batch mining CLI** — `mine_github.py --batch` processes multiple repos from a JSON config, producing per-repo JSONL files ready for S3 upload
- **Phase 1 repos config** — 10 curated repos across Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go with quality filtering defaults

### Training pair structure
Each pair encodes one step for the hypernetwork:
- **activation_text**: `## Task` + `## Current Code` (previous diff) + `## Review Feedback`
- **teacher_text**: activation + `## Revision` (the correct code change)

### New files
- `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_diff.py` (diff compressor)
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_diff.py` (9 tests, 100% coverage)
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_mine_batch.py` (2 tests)
- `instructions/mining_repos.json` (Phase 1: 10 repos)

### Modified files
- `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py` (+normalize_mined_pairs)
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_data.py` (+9 tests)
- `scripts/mine_github.py` (batch mode: --batch, --output-dir)

### Usage
```bash
# Batch mine Phase 1 repos (~500 PRs, ~1500 training pairs)
uv run python scripts/mine_github.py \
  --batch instructions/mining_repos.json \
  --output-dir data/pairs/

# Upload to S3
aws s3 sync data/pairs/ $RUNE_TRAINING_DATA/
```

## Test plan
- [x] 149 tests pass, 0 regressions (`uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/ -v`)
- [x] d2l_diff.py at 100% coverage, d2l_data.py at 93%
- [x] ruff check + format clean on all files
- [x] E2E verified: mined fastapi/fastapi PR #12101 → 5 training pairs with correct structure
- [ ] Run full Phase 1 mining on CPU machine with GitHub token

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1

"""Thin GitHub REST API client with auth, pagination, and rate-limit retry.

Designed for batch data mining on a training VM (sync httpx is fine).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any

import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

__all__ = ["GitHubClient"]

_LINK_NEXT_RE = re.compile(r'<([^>]+)>;\s*rel="next"')


class GitHubClient:
    """Minimal GitHub REST API client.

    Handles authentication, paginated list endpoints, and automatic
    retry on rate-limit 403 responses.

    Args:
        token: GitHub personal access token. Optional for public
            endpoints but required for private repos and higher rate
            limits.
        base_url: API base URL. Override for GitHub Enterprise.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        token: str | None = None,
        base_url: str = "https://api.github.com",
    ) -> None:
        """Initialize the client with optional auth token.

        Args:
            token: GitHub personal access token.
            base_url: API base URL.
        """
        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self._headers: dict[str, str] = {
            "Accept": "application/vnd.github+json",
            "X-GitHub-Api-Version": "2022-11-28",
        }
        if token is not None:
            self._headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {token}"

<<<<<<< HEAD
    def _get_response(
        self,
        url: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> httpx.Response:
        """GET with rate-limit retry, returning the raw response.

        Args:
            url: Full URL to request.
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            The successful httpx.Response.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp
        # Unreachable in normal flow, but satisfies type checker.
        resp.raise_for_status()  # pragma: no cover
        return resp  # pragma: no cover

=======

    ) -> Any:
        """GET a single API endpoint with rate-limit retry.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url (e.g. ``/repos/owner/repo``).
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            Parsed JSON response body.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        url = f"{self._base_url}{path}"
<<<<<<< HEAD
        return self._get_response(url, params=params, max_retries=max_retries).json()

                url,

            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except Exception:
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(

                        wait,

                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue

            return resp.json()
        # Unreachable in normal flow, but satisfies type checker.
        resp.raise_for_status()  # pragma: no cover
        return resp.json()  # pragma: no cover
>>>>>>> 986dba6 (feat: add GitHubClient with auth, pagination, and rate-limit retry)

    def get_paginated(
        self,
        path: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_pages: int = 10,
        per_page: int = 100,
    ) -> list[Any]:
        """GET a paginated list endpoint, following Link rel=next headers.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url.
            params: Optional query parameters (``per_page`` is injected).
            max_pages: Maximum number of pages to fetch.
            per_page: Items per page (max 100 for most GitHub endpoints).

        Returns:
            Flat list of all items across all fetched pages.
        """
        merged_params: dict[str, Any] = dict(params or {})
        merged_params["per_page"] = per_page

        items: list[Any] = []
        url: str | None = f"{self._base_url}{path}"

        for _ in range(max_pages):
            if url is None:
                break

            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
>>>>>>> 986dba6 (feat: add GitHubClient with auth, pagination, and rate-limit retry)
            items.extend(resp.json())

            # After the first request, params are baked into the Link URL.
            merged_params = {}

            link = resp.headers.get("Link", "")
            match = _LINK_NEXT_RE.search(link)
            url = match.group(1) if match else None

        return items

input.command:
gh pr close 18 --comment "Superseded by PR from clean branch (no merge conflicts)" 2>&1 && gh pr create --base main --head feat/training-dataset-pipeline --title "feat: training dataset pipeline for hypernetwork (diff compression, pair extraction, batch mining)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **d2l_diff.py** — RTK-style diff compression: filters lockfiles, generated code, binary files, and build artifacts from mined diffs
- **normalize_mined_pairs()** — Extracts per-step training pairs from mined PR trajectories (review feedback → code revision cycles), producing `{task_id, activation_text, teacher_text, metadata}` records compatible with the existing distillation pipeline
- **Batch mining CLI** — `mine_github.py --batch` processes multiple repos from a JSON config, producing per-repo JSONL files ready for S3 upload
- **Phase 1 repos config** — 10 curated repos across Python, TypeScript, Rust, and Go

### Training pair structure
Each pair encodes one step for the hypernetwork:
- **activation_text**: `## Task` + `## Current Code` (previous diff) + `## Review Feedback`
- **teacher_text**: activation + `## Revision` (the correct code change)

### Usage
```bash
# Batch mine Phase 1 repos
uv run python scripts/mine_github.py \
  --batch instructions/mining_repos.json \
  --output-dir data/pairs/

# Upload to S3
aws s3 sync data/pairs/ $RUNE_TRAINING_DATA/
```

## Test plan
- [x] 34 new tests pass (9 d2l_diff, 9 normalize_mined_pairs, 2 batch mining, 14 existing)
- [x] d2l_diff.py at 100% coverage
- [x] ruff check + format clean
- [x] E2E verified: mined fastapi/fastapi PR #12101 → 5 training pairs with correct structure
- [ ] Run full Phase 1 mining on CPU machine

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1

content.text: GitHub returned 503s on both — likely from parallel requests. The `GitHubClient` only retries 403 (rate limit), not 503 (server error). Let me add a quick retry for 503s, then re-run sequentially.

content.content:
54	    def _get_response(
55	        self,
56	        url: str,
57	        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
58	        max_retries: int = 3,
59	    ) -> httpx.Response:
60	        """GET with rate-limit retry, returning the raw response.
61	
62	        Args:
63	            url: Full URL to request.
64	            params: Optional query parameters.
65	            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.
66	
67	        Returns:
68	            The successful httpx.Response.
69	
70	        Raises:
71	            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
72	        """
73	        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
74	            resp = httpx.get(
75	                url,
76	                headers=self._headers,
77	                params=params,
78	                timeout=30.0,
79	            )
80	            if resp.status_code == 403:
81	                try:
82	                    body = resp.json()
83	                except (ValueError, KeyError):
84	                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
85	                    body = {}
86	                message = body.get("message", "")
87	                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
88	                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
89	                    logger.warning(
90	                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
91	                        wait,
92	                        attempt + 1,

def _get_response(
        self,
        url: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> httpx.Response:
        """GET with rate-limit retry, returning the raw response.

        Args:
            url: Full URL to request.
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            The successful httpx.Response.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp
        # Unreachable in normal flow, but satisfies type checker.
        resp.raise_for_status()  # pragma: no cover
        return resp  # pragma: no cover

            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on transient errors.

        Returns:
            The successful httpx.Response.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-retryable error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            if resp.status_code >= 500 and attempt < max_retries:
                wait = 2 ** attempt
                logger.warning(
                    "Server error %d, retrying in %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                    resp.status_code,
                    wait,
                    attempt + 1,
                    max_retries,
                )
                time.sleep(wait)
                continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp

"""Thin GitHub REST API client with auth, pagination, and rate-limit retry.

Designed for batch data mining on a training VM (sync httpx is fine).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any

import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

__all__ = ["GitHubClient"]

_LINK_NEXT_RE = re.compile(r'<([^>]+)>;\s*rel="next"')


class GitHubClient:
    """Minimal GitHub REST API client.

    Handles authentication, paginated list endpoints, and automatic
    retry on rate-limit 403 responses.

    Args:
        token: GitHub personal access token. Optional for public
            endpoints but required for private repos and higher rate
            limits.
        base_url: API base URL. Override for GitHub Enterprise.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        token: str | None = None,
        base_url: str = "https://api.github.com",
    ) -> None:
        """Initialize the client with optional auth token.

        Args:
            token: GitHub personal access token.
            base_url: API base URL.
        """
        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self._headers: dict[str, str] = {
            "Accept": "application/vnd.github+json",
            "X-GitHub-Api-Version": "2022-11-28",
        }
        if token is not None:
            self._headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {token}"

    def _get_response(
        self,
        url: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> httpx.Response:
        """GET with rate-limit retry, returning the raw response.

        Args:
            url: Full URL to request.
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            The successful httpx.Response.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp
        # Unreachable in normal flow, but satisfies type checker.
        resp.raise_for_status()  # pragma: no cover
        return resp  # pragma: no cover

    def get(
        self,
        path: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> Any:
        """GET a single API endpoint with rate-limit retry.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url (e.g. ``/repos/owner/repo``).
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            Parsed JSON response body.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        url = f"{self._base_url}{path}"
        return self._get_response(url, params=params, max_retries=max_retries).json()

    def get_paginated(
        self,
        path: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_pages: int = 10,
        per_page: int = 100,
    ) -> list[Any]:
        """GET a paginated list endpoint, following Link rel=next headers.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url.
            params: Optional query parameters (``per_page`` is injected).
            max_pages: Maximum number of pages to fetch.
            per_page: Items per page (max 100 for most GitHub endpoints).

        Returns:
            Flat list of all items across all fetched pages.
        """
        merged_params: dict[str, Any] = dict(params or {})
        merged_params["per_page"] = per_page

        items: list[Any] = []
        url: str | None = f"{self._base_url}{path}"

        for _ in range(max_pages):
            if url is None:
                break
            resp = self._get_response(url, params=merged_params)
            items.extend(resp.json())

            # After the first request, params are baked into the Link URL.
            merged_params = {}

            link = resp.headers.get("Link", "")
            match = _LINK_NEXT_RE.search(link)
            url = match.group(1) if match else None

        return items

            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Thin GitHub REST API client with auth, pagination, and rate-limit retry.

Designed for batch data mining on a training VM (sync httpx is fine).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import re
import time
from typing import Any

import httpx

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

__all__ = ["GitHubClient"]

_LINK_NEXT_RE = re.compile(r'<([^>]+)>;\s*rel="next"')


class GitHubClient:
    """Minimal GitHub REST API client.

    Handles authentication, paginated list endpoints, and automatic
    retry on rate-limit 403 responses.

    Args:
        token: GitHub personal access token. Optional for public
            endpoints but required for private repos and higher rate
            limits.
        base_url: API base URL. Override for GitHub Enterprise.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        token: str | None = None,
        base_url: str = "https://api.github.com",
    ) -> None:
        """Initialize the client with optional auth token.

        Args:
            token: GitHub personal access token.
            base_url: API base URL.
        """
        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
        self._headers: dict[str, str] = {
            "Accept": "application/vnd.github+json",
            "X-GitHub-Api-Version": "2022-11-28",
        }
        if token is not None:
            self._headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {token}"

    def _get_response(
        self,
        url: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> httpx.Response:
        """GET with rate-limit retry, returning the raw response.

        Args:
            url: Full URL to request.
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            The successful httpx.Response.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        for attempt in range(max_retries + 1):
            resp = httpx.get(
                url,
                headers=self._headers,
                params=params,
                timeout=30.0,
            )
            if resp.status_code == 403:
                try:
                    body = resp.json()
                except (ValueError, KeyError):
                    logger.debug("Non-JSON 403 body, skip rate-limit check")
                    body = {}
                message = body.get("message", "")
                if "rate limit" in message.lower() and attempt < max_retries:
                    wait = int(resp.headers.get("Retry-After", "60"))
                    logger.warning(
                        "Rate limited, sleeping %ds (attempt %d/%d)",
                        wait,
                        attempt + 1,
                        max_retries,
                    )
                    time.sleep(wait)
                    continue
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp
        # Unreachable in normal flow, but satisfies type checker.
        resp.raise_for_status()  # pragma: no cover
        return resp  # pragma: no cover

    def get(
        self,
        path: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_retries: int = 3,
    ) -> Any:
        """GET a single API endpoint with rate-limit retry.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url (e.g. ``/repos/owner/repo``).
            params: Optional query parameters.
            max_retries: Maximum number of retries on rate-limit 403.

        Returns:
            Parsed JSON response body.

        Raises:
            httpx.HTTPStatusError: On non-rate-limit error responses.
        """
        url = f"{self._base_url}{path}"
        return self._get_response(url, params=params, max_retries=max_retries).json()

    def get_paginated(
        self,
        path: str,
        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
        max_pages: int = 10,
        per_page: int = 100,
    ) -> list[Any]:
        """GET a paginated list endpoint, following Link rel=next headers.

        Args:
            path: API path relative to base_url.
            params: Optional query parameters (``per_page`` is injected).
            max_pages: Maximum number of pages to fetch.
            per_page: Items per page (max 100 for most GitHub endpoints).

        Returns:
            Flat list of all items across all fetched pages.
        """
        merged_params: dict[str, Any] = dict(params or {})
        merged_params["per_page"] = per_page

        items: list[Any] = []
        url: str | None = f"{self._base_url}{path}"

        for _ in range(max_pages):
            if url is None:
                break
            resp = self._get_response(url, params=merged_params)
            items.extend(resp.json())

            # After the first request, params are baked into the Link URL.
            merged_params = {}

            link = resp.headers.get("Link", "")
            match = _LINK_NEXT_RE.search(link)
            url = match.group(1) if match else None

        return items
